name: Apple-Walnut Muffins
recipeIngredient: 2 medium apples, peeled, cored and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Peel, seed, and finely chop the apples. Grease 12 muffin cups or line them with paper liners. Set aside.
3. In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, eggs, apple juice concentrate, melted butter, and vanilla extract. Stir until just combined.
4. Fold in chopped apples and walnuts. Spoon batter into prepared cups, filling each about 3/4 full.
5. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown. Let stand in the pan on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Remove muffins from the pan.
6. Serve warm or at room temperature.
7. Cooled muffins may be wrapped securely and frozen up to 3 months.
Nutrition information per muffin: 199 calories, 4 gm protein, 25 gm carbohydrate, 9 gm fat, 49 mg cholesterol, 183 mg sodium, 1/4 diabetic starch/bread exchange, 1-3/4 diabetic fat exchange, 1/2 diabetic fruit exchange.
